title: FiLM global conditioning (Re/AoA/NACA) via DiT-style scale+shift on attn+MLP, zero-init
notes: Strongest signal of round 3: -10.6% vs new anchor 47.3987. All 4 splits improve. Conflicts with #1093 squash; sent back for rebase. Originally committed test_avg/mae_surf_p is NaN (upstream scoring bug); post-fix rerun line provides clean test numbers. Includes a defensive non-finite-y filter in train.py (alternate fix for the same bug frieren caught).

title: Fourier positional encoding on (x,z) at freqs {1,2,4,8,16}*pi
notes: Beats reference baseline 47.7385 by 5.05%; conflicts with #1093 squash; sent back for rebase. Includes NaN guard fix in evaluate_split for non-finite ground-truth samples (sample 20 of test_geom_camber_cruise).

title: slice_num sweep {32,64,128} on compound baseline
notes: Ran on OLD compound baseline without --fourier_pos_enc. Result 47.3550 does not beat current baseline 44.4154. slice_num=32 is worse than slice_num=64.

title: slice_num sweep {32,64,128} on compound baseline
notes: Ran on OLD compound baseline without --fourier_pos_enc. Best of 3 slice_num variants; result 47.2312 does not beat current baseline 44.4154. Confirms slice_num=64 as Pareto-optimal at current model capacity.

title: slice_num sweep {32,64,128} on compound baseline
notes: Ran on OLD compound baseline without --fourier_pos_enc. Worst of 3 slice_num variants — more VRAM, slower, and worse accuracy. slice_num=128 clearly ruled out.

title: FiLM global conditioning + Fourier pos enc on current best baseline
notes: STRONG WINNER pending rebase. -7.5% vs current best 43.9575 (40.6661, delta=-3.2914). FiLM zero-init (weight=0, bias=0 on final projections) MUST be re-applied AFTER self.apply(self._init_weights) at lines 246-249 in train.py. Best epoch=50 (cap, not converged) — extended training expected to push further. val_geom_camber_rc improved only -0.3% (57.74->57.55), the hardest OOD split barely responding to FiLM — needs targeted mechanism. Rebase conflict detected; sent back for rebase onto post-#1148 tip with --fourier_freqs 1 2 4 8 16 32 64.
